Token DNA: Neutral tokens, semantic scales, strong primitives, dark/light parity
Typography DNA: Functional sans, clear hierarchy, code-friendly support
Surface Treatment: Low-noise surfaces, subtle borders, restrained shadows
Component DNA: Buttons/inputs/cards feel infrastructural rather than decorative
Steal Carefully: Token discipline, information density, primitive layering, icon consistency
Avoid When: You need playful, expressive, campaign-style marketing pages

Token DNA: High-contrast semantic tokens, black/white led, accent restraint
Typography DNA: Geist Sans/Mono pairing, sharp developer voice
Surface Treatment: Clean planes, thin borders, quiet depth
Component DNA: Components feel polished but minimal, fast to scan
Strengths: Beautiful dev-tool aesthetic, modern polish, readable docs
Steal Carefully: Grid feel, contrast discipline, type hierarchy, premium restraint
Avoid When: You need playful mass-market consumer energy

Typography DNA: Functional commerce typography, clarity over flair
Surface Treatment: Soft surfaces, reliable contrast, utility-led treatment
Component DNA: Forms, tables, filters, navigation optimized for long sessions
Motion DNA: Helpful motion, visible state changes, minimal spectacle
Strengths: Operational clarity, dense workflows, onboarding consistency
Steal Carefully: Workflow ergonomics, state patterns, table/form behavior
Avoid When: You need edgy brand-heavy creative direction

Token DNA: Token-first, accessible color system, utility discipline
Surface Treatment: Minimal decoration, clear boundaries, strong defaults
Component DNA: Components built for clarity, forms, alerts, navigation
Steal Carefully: Pattern guidance, content structure, mobile resilience, form usability
Avoid When: You want luxurious or highly expressive brand feel

Surface Treatment: Quiet surfaces with strong state clarity
Component DNA: Composable accessible primitives, stable interaction model
Motion DNA: Minimal but polished motion tied to interaction states
Strengths: Fast implementation, low drift, maintainable scale
Steal Carefully: Primitive architecture, interaction states, accessible composition
Avoid When: You want distinct brand character out of the box

Product Focus: Modern app UI, starter systems, custom product UI
Spacing Rhythm: Depends on adopter; usually balanced modern spacing
Typography DNA: Usually Inter-like modern sans with practical hierarchy
Surface Treatment: Default modern SaaS surfaces, easy to restyle
Component DNA: Component recipes you own and mutate
Motion DNA: Minimal default motion; motion is yours to define
Strengths: Open code, speed, ownership, easy customization
Steal Carefully: Ownership model, copy-and-own workflow, pragmatic defaults
Avoid When: You need strong taste without adding your own design direction

Product Focus: Marketing sites, SaaS landing pages, app shells
Spacing Rhythm: Strong section rhythm, padding discipline, reliable responsiveness
Typography DNA: Modern sans hierarchy with marketing clarity
Motion DNA: Tasteful hover and reveal motion, pattern-driven
Steal Carefully: Section sequencing, responsive composition, CTA structure
Avoid When: You blindly paste patterns without unifying tokens/taste

Token DNA: Algorithmic color roles, semantic elevation, state layers
Component DNA: Rich component system for app flows
Strengths: Excellent breadth, adaptive tokens, platform guidance
Steal Carefully: State layers, touch ergonomics, comprehensive tokens
Avoid When: You want boutique brand distinctiveness over universal patterns

Token DNA: Enterprise semantic tokens, solid state communication, scale-ready components
Spacing Rhythm: Moderate density with strong task grouping
Typography DNA: Readable product sans with strong UI labels and support copy
Surface Treatment: Clear layers, friendly panels, strong navigation treatment
Component DNA: Tables, boards, forms, nav, and productivity surfaces tuned for team workflows
Strengths: Complex workflow support, enterprise consistency, component breadth
Steal Carefully: Information architecture, team-workflow clarity, state communication

Token DNA: Comprehensive semantic token model with strong component coverage
Typography DNA: Functional enterprise typography with clear label/data distinction
Surface Treatment: Layered surfaces and obvious component boundaries
Component DNA: Data tables, forms, filters, navs, and enterprise widgets at scale
Strengths: Broad enterprise coverage, admin maturity, consistent forms/tables
Steal Carefully: Dense admin ergonomics, form/table systemization, enterprise states
Avoid When: You want warm editorial minimalism or strong brand storytelling

Product Focus: Enterprise software, data-heavy products, system tools
Token DNA: Tokenized design language with deep state semantics and multi-theme support
Spacing Rhythm: Tight and systematic, optimized for complex interfaces
Typography DNA: Structured typography with strong information hierarchy
Surface Treatment: Measured surfaces, clear divisions, low-noise depth
Component DNA: Enterprise components for dense workflows, data, and structured interactions
Strengths: Great for serious data products and enterprise rigor
Steal Carefully: Density control, serious tone, accessibility discipline
Avoid When: You need playful consumer warmth or soft-future expressiveness

Product Focus: Productivity apps, enterprise suites, desktop-web ecosystems
Token DNA: Systematic tokens, platform-adaptive color and depth rules
Spacing Rhythm: Balanced spacing that works across desktop-heavy patterns
Typography DNA: Clear product typography with practical scaling
Surface Treatment: Soft depth, layered panels, adaptive surfaces
Component DNA: Enterprise productivity components, nav systems, dialogs, and workspace patterns
Strengths: Good for cross-platform productivity and office-like environments
Steal Carefully: Adaptive workspace patterns, desktop-first productivity ergonomics
Avoid When: You want niche boutique product personality

Token DNA: Primitive-level tokens and semantics with styling freedom
Typography DNA: Typography intentionally left to the adopting product system
Surface Treatment: No default taste, just primitive structure
Component DNA: Behavioral component foundations with low imposed styling
Strengths: Excellent base for owning behavior without inheriting visual baggage
Avoid When: You need finished visual taste out of the box

Product Focus: Modern SaaS products, internal systems, product design teams
Token DNA: Design-token-first with strong product UI defaults
Typography DNA: Modern product typography with strong hierarchy
Surface Treatment: Calm surfaces, refined shadows, coherent borders
Component DNA: Well-shaped product components with thoughtful defaults
Strengths: Strong middle ground between taste and practicality
Steal Carefully: Coherent defaults, product-system thinking, maintainable polish
Avoid When: You want highly experimental or brand-heavy visuals

Product Focus: Developer platform, data products, B2B infrastructure
Token DNA: Muted technical palette with disciplined accent strategy
Typography DNA: Developer-friendly typography with strong technical readability
Surface Treatment: Clean panels, restrained marketing surfaces, proof-oriented layouts
Component DNA: Platform blocks, diagrams, docs-like marketing, trustworthy forms
Strengths: Great for technical platform trust and infra storytelling
